intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards prioritize prominent metric display over explanatory narrative, even when lacking data interpretation support
facade.label: Big numbers, tiny context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should lead with large, attention-grabbing KPIs (weight, steps, sleep hours) with minimal surrounding explanation or trend context
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should embed metrics within narrative frames—each number paired with interpretation, status badge, or mini-story of what it means for the user's experiment

evidence.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards prioritize prominent metric display over explanatory narrative, even when lacking data interpretation support
evidence.implication: Dashboard should lead with large, attention-grabbing KPIs (weight, steps, sleep hours) with minimal surrounding explanation or trend context

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual transitions that reveal data incrementally rather than instant page loads, creating a sense of discovery in health tracking
facade.label: Smooth scroll fades
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use scroll-triggered animations, fade-in effects on metric cards, and progressive data reveal as user scrolls through experiment history
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should load all data instantly, use no scroll animations, and display metrics in static grids without transition effects

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments may prefer calm, understated visual palettes that feel meditative rather than clinical or energetic
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use desaturated pastels, warm neutrals, or low-contrast earth tones to create a soothing, introspective mood that doesn't overwhelm during frequent check-ins
facade.rejectImplies: The user prefers either cooler, more saturated colors OR higher contrast and visual pop to keep the dashboard feeling active and engaging rather than calming

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking multiple health experiments need a persistent navigation axis (sidebar) paired with card-based content layout to balance quick access to experiments with detailed metric viewing
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User favors persistent, always-visible experiment list with modular content blocks; likely values quick switching and organized visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based or full-width list navigation; may want less screen real estate devoted to navigation or denser information presentation

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable shapes over clinical or institutional aesthetics
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use curved UI elements, buttons, cards with generous border-radius, avoiding hard 90-degree angles to feel supportive rather than sterile
facade.rejectImplies: User favors precision and clarity through sharp edges, clean lines, minimal decoration—prefers a more technical or data-focused visual language

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, coaching voice over clinical language when tracking personal health experiments
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Build with encouraging, accessible language—celebrate small wins, use 'you' and 'we', reduce jargon
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward professional, data-focused voice—clinical terminology, neutral stance, numbers-first presentation

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards value narrative progression (how metrics changed over time) more than static point-in-time values, especially when experimenting with behavior changes
facade.label: Timeline, not numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should emphasize trend lines, before/after comparisons, and temporal context over isolated KPI callouts. Users want to see their story, not just their stats.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prioritize instant metric recognition and current state over understanding change. Dashboard design should minimize time-based storytelling in favor of immediate, glanceable numbers.

draft.title: HealthLab Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics overview, and timeline view. Users can log daily observations and see patterns emerge over weeks and months.
